channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Party City going out of business, starting process 'immediately': report
item.description: Party City, once among the top names in party-supply retailers, is going out of business, according to an announcement from CEO Barry Litwin that was viewed by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Traveling? You can still watch NewsNation
item.description: The holiday season means millions of Americans will be traveling to see family across the country. But what if your relatives don’t have cable? How will you watch NewsNation? Thankfully, you’ve got options.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Crowds in Mayotte vent frustration with cyclone response as Macron tours devastation
item.description: MIRERENI, Mayotte (AP) — Crowds in Mayotte vented their frustration at French President Emmanuel Macron, with some booing, as he toured destruction wrought by the strongest cyclone to hit the French territory in nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wisconsin police chief overseeing school shooting probe is named to Seattle job
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Madison Police Chief Shon Barnes was named as the chief of police in Seattle on Friday, five days after he was thrust into the national spotlight because of a shooting at a religious school in Wisconsin that killed a teacher and student.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER and TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada's Trudeau reshuffles his Cabinet as resignation calls mount and discontent rises
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's chances of staying on in power have become more tenuous after the opposition party that backed his government for years announced it will vote no confidence in the government when Parliament resumes.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Indiana man sentenced to the maximum of 130 years in prison for 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike was sentenced to a maximum of 130 years in prison Friday in the case that’s long cast a shadow over the teens’ small hometown of Delphi. Allen, 52, was convicted on Nov.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah girl paralyzed by stray bullet gets a heartwarming surprise
item.description: It's the season of giving and what better way to give back than to surprise one very deserving Utah girl?
item.dc:creator: Jillian Smukler, KREX 5

item.title: Murder and terrorism charges brought against Iranian officer in 2022 killing of American in Iraq
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An Iranian officer blamed for the 2022 killing of an American in Iraq has been charged in New York with federal murder and terrorism crimes, authorities announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump adds Europe to the list of US trade partners he's threatening with tariffs
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday added the 27 countries that make up the European Union to the list of trade partners he's threatening with tariffs — unless the group takes steps to import more U.S. goods.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US flu season is underway, as cases surge in some areas and vaccinations lag
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. flu season is underway, with cases surging across much of the country, health officials said Friday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention noted sharp increases in several measures, including lab tests and emergency room visits.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US civil rights probe faults Philadelphia school district over handling of antisemitism reports
item.description: Federal education officials say the Philadelphia school district failed to show it protected Jewish students from harassment despite “repeated, extensive notice” that students, teachers and administrators were engaging in antisemitic behavior.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rubinkam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Leap of faith: A few young women in US buck the trends by joining the ranks of Catholic nuns
item.description: PALMYRA, Pa. (AP) — It was a radical idea, and for many years she had been resistant. But as Zoey Stapleton, 24, walked down a darkened hiking trail, steps behind her parents, she counted back from three and “took a leap of faith,” revealing to them that she wanted to become a nun.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More student loans canceled in final round of forgiveness before Biden leaves office
item.description: With the latest round of relief, Biden has now canceled an unprecedented $180 billion in federal student loans through existing programs, covering 4.9 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thief returns baby Jesus figurine stolen from Colorado nativity scene
item.description: Fort Collins police were looking for a suspect who reportedly stole a baby Jesus figurine from a nativity scene, but said the thief returned the figurine on Thursday with a brief note.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: FDA updates definition of 'healthy' on food labels for first time in 30 years
item.description: A new rule from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) will update what it means for food to be labeled “healthy” for the first time in 30 years, a move that aligns with current nutrition science and is meant to help consumers better navigate food labels.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, KRON4

item.title: Snow, ice and strong winds make for dangerous conditions in upper Midwest
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A fast-moving winter storm brought snow, ice, strong winds and bitter cold to much of the upper Midwest, snarling traffic in the Twin Cities and forcing North Dakota officials to shut down an interstate highway.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In Florida, a race is on to save the Everglades and protect a key source of drinking water
item.description: EVERGLADES, Fla. (AP) — In a region of Florida known as the River of Grass, John Kominoski plops into hip-deep waters. Blobs of brown periphyton – a mishmash of algae, bacteria and other organisms – carpet the surface.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and REBECCA BLACKWELL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Uvalde schools police chief loses bid to toss criminal charges related to 2022 shooting
item.description: UVALDE, Texas (AP) — A Texas judge on Thursday refused to throw out criminal charges accusing the former Uvalde schools police chief of putting children at risk during the slow response to the 2022 Robb Elementary School shooting, while a lawyer for his co-defendant said they want to move the upcomi
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House GOP eyes plan B stopgap with separate 2-part debt limit commitment
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing an alternative government funding stopgap that excludes a debt ceiling increase, and are instead working on a commitment to raise the borrowing limit twice next year in a bid to appease President-elect Trump, two sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: France rape trial: Gisèle Pelicot speaks out after ex-husband, 50 others found guilty
item.description: Gisèle Pelicot spoke of her “very difficult ordeal” after 51 men were all found guilty Thursday in the drugging-and-rape trial that turned her into a feminist hero, expressing support for other victims of sexual violence whose cases don't get such attention and “whose stories remain untold.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead, 9 injured in Maryland shooting and fiery car crash: police
item.description: One person has died and nine others were injured following a shooting and related fiery car crash in Maryland on Tuesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, WGN 9

item.title: Trump names former football star as ambassador to Bahamas
item.description: (The Hill) - President-elect Trump said Tuesday he would nominate former NFL player and one-time Georgia Senate candidate Herschel Walker to serve as his ambassador to the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KTLA 5

item.title: Electoral College formally votes to confirm Trump's win
item.description: President-elect Trump has clinched the necessary number of Electoral College votes to become the next occupant of the White House, teeing up the official certification of his victory before Congress next month.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Attack on 2 apparent Venezuelan migrants in Denver area seems gang-related, police chief says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — More than a dozen people tied up, pistol whipped and terrorized a man and woman at a crime-riddled apartment complex in a Denver suburb, the city’s police chief said Tuesday, adding it appeared to be a gang-related attack on two Venezuelan immigrants.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Congress strikes deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders have struck a bipartisan deal to keep the government funded beyond a looming Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Why shooting down mystery drones carries big risks
item.description: The buzz around the mysterious flurry of drones above New Jersey and other nearby states has prompted some officials, including President-elect Trump, to call for the U.S. military to shoot down the mystery objects. But such measures come with major risks, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, WGN 9

item.title: Shooting in Madison is the latest carnage at a US school or college over the past 25 years
item.description: Hundreds of people in the U.S. have died in shootings over the past 25 years in places like offices, stores and theaters, but the carnage perhaps hits hardest when it happens in schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart testing body cameras for some in-store employees
item.description: Some in-store Walmart employees are wearing body cameras as part of a pilot program, according to a company spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump sued the Des Moines Register and its pollster for “brazen election interference” in publishing a survey the weekend before the election that showed Democrat Kamala Harris with a surprising lead of three percentage points in the state. The Register's parent Gannett Co.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: How to claim a 2nd payment from the Equifax data breach settlement
item.description: Equifax has started alerting people of more money waiting for them from a massive fund the credit reporting agency paid to settle a data breach.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect charged with killing UnitedHealthcare CEO as an act of terrorism, authorities announce
item.description: Mangione had previously been charged with murder in Thompson's killing, but the terror allegation is new.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the victims in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A 14-year-old girl and a substitute teacher were killed in Monday's shooting. Another teacher and five students were injured.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC
